title: Troubleshooting, Security, Recovery, and Operations Handoff · System Forensics
summary: Resolve a multi-layer workstation incident and hand off a safe, reproducible operating procedure to another technician. Learners isolate plausible failures, compare competing hypotheses, and record cause-level repair evidence. Planned artifact: Repair a changed failure in a complete Linux operations evidence portfolio and publish the reproduction, evidence, repair, and regression record. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence…
objectives: Form competing hypotheses, select discriminating observations, and update confidence without changing multiple layers at once.
Use privilege only for the smallest justified operation while preserving command provenance, environment boundaries, and audit evidence.
Preview scope, snapshot relevant state, define stop conditions, and test rollback before risky filesystem, package, or service changes.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.shell:
# linux-basics cumulative operations evidence notebook
# Commands run only in the deterministic stateful terminal simulator.
# Record hypothesis, command, expected signal, observation, conclusion, and recovery.

steps.title: Inspect the failure record
steps.instruction:
debug case — A debug Linux incident crosses filesystem, permission, process, service, and network evidence. Preserve a baseline, change one bounded layer, record exit status, and verify rollback on case 3.

Inspect the Hypothesis Ladder trace for this debug case. Which record connects a precondition, observed state change, and boundary result?
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for form competing hypotheses, select discriminating observations, and update confidence without changing multiple layers at once.

content.text: For Hypothesis Ladder, distinguish a claim from evidence by locating the relevant precondition, trace or invariant, and changed input result.

hints: Name the Hypothesis Ladder precondition or invariant that the debug case must preserve.

Trace this concrete case before choosing: A debug Linux incident crosses filesystem, permission, process, service, and network evidence. Preserve a baseline, change one bounded layer, record exit status, and verify rollback on case 3.
Use the documented misconception as a counterexample target: Trying plausible fixes quickly is equivalent to diagnosis.

steps.title: Implement the cause-level repair
steps.instruction: Append a safe Linux operations investigation headed "# Evidence: linux-operations-handoff-14ug5o" with a concrete hypothesis, one bounded simulator command, and an observation that changes the next decision.
steps.why: Building a runnable or mechanically reviewable artifact turns Operations Handoff into observable performance evidence.

# observation: [replace with the result that changes the next decision]

options.text: Capture user, directory, environment, target state, and authoritative command behavior
Predict shell parsing, expansion, permissions, side effects, exit status, and safety boundary
Run the smallest simulated observation or reversible change and retain exact output
Verify changed state, failure path, rollback, and an accessible operations handoff

steps.title: Publish the incident record
steps.instruction: Write the debug evidence note for Operations Handoff: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — A debug Linux incident crosses filesystem, permission, process, service, and network evidence. Preserve a baseline, change one bounded layer, record exit status, and verify rollback on case 6.
steps.why: Retrieval and explanation expose gaps that recognition questions can hide.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Write an accessible runbook with prerequisites, exact observations, safe actions, expected signals, exceptions, recovery, and ownership.
